title: hieracium pilosella proles peleterianum
intro: Hieracium pilosella proles peleterianum, also known as the Pilosella peleteriana, is a subspecies of the Hieracium pilosella plant, commonly referred to as the mouse-ear hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella peleteriana has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for rheumatism and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Pilosella peleteriana can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, and is used to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
